hook: The Universe is 13.8 Billion Years Old.
facts: Light travels at a finite speed (approx. 300,000 km/s).
So, the maximum distance light could travel to us since the Big Bang is 13.8 billion light-years.
You'd expect our 'observable universe' to be 27.6 billion light-years across (diameter).
payoff: But due to the universe's constant expansion, the objects at the edge of our observable universe are actually an astounding 93 BILLION light-years away from us!
title: 🤯 The Universe is WAY BIGGER Than You Think!

facts: A single cubic centimeter (cm³) of Earth's atmosphere holds ~2.5 x 10¹⁹ molecules.
The best vacuum labs on Earth can achieve contains ~10,000 molecules per cubic centimeter.
In the 'empty' void between stars, the average density drops to just ~1 hydrogen atom per cubic centimeter.
But in the vast intergalactic voids, it's a staggering ~1 atom per *cubic meter*.
payoff: The average intergalactic void is 1,000,000 times emptier than interstellar space and far beyond any vacuum we can create!
title: Space isn't just empty, it's INCREDIBLY empty! 🤯
